text:
Furious Parisians are threatening to defecate in the Seine in protest at the billions spent cleaning up the river before the Olympics, forcing the city's mayor to postpone her swim in the water to prove it is safe to another date.

The mayor of the French capital has postponed her plunge into the Seine after furious Parisians threatened to defecate in it to protest against the billions spent cleaning the iconic river ahead of the Olympics next month.

Parisian officials have scrambled to ensure the Seine is up to hygienic standard to ensure Olympians can compete in swimming events in the river once the games begin on July 26.

In recent months, E. Coli bacteria, as well as other pathogens, have been detected in the Seine - raising fears that Olympic swimmers could be infected with potentially deadly dermatological and gastrointestinal diseases.

As a result, many Olympians and people involved in the competition have raised concerns about the water quality.

On May 23, just a few minutes after Ms Hidalgo said she would dive into the Seine, the hashtag #JechiedanslaSeinele23juin began to circulate on X.

Translated to “I s**t in the Seine on June 23”, the hashtag has been shared tens of thousands of times.

A collective was quickly established, with a young computer engineer living in the Paris region setting up a website on the evening of May 23.

The website allowed people to calculate where and when to “do their business” so it would arrive in Paris by the specified date.

The anonymous programmer told Actu Paris it started out as a “joke”, but now people really were going to “s**t in the Seine, or set up militant actions”.

“Nothing is excluded,” he told the outlet.

On its website, a political slogan has been used among the collective protesters online that reads:  “Because after putting us in shit it's up to them to bathe in our shit.”

“In terms of France's image, it's good that the Seine is clean,” the computer engineer said.

“The problem is that all the resources that have been invested have not been to resolve all the social problems we have at the moment.

“We have the feeling of being abandoned. We see where their priority was.”

Here's maybe a better perspective...No wonder Hidalgo wasn't having any part of swimming in that...#JeChieDansLaSeineLe23Juinpic.twitter.com/Nh5jcMILs9

Since 2015, €1.4 billion, or AUD$2.25 billion has been spent as part of the river clean-up operation.

The first deputy mayor of Paris Emmanuel Grégoire said the protest was not being taken “very seriously” and that it was “nothing more than a joke”.

“Beyond the mockery, the idea is to show that the people in charge are going to swim. This is the strength of example,” Mr Grégoire.

But Ms Hidalgo was not taking any chances and has postponed her dive until after the French elections in July.

I almost feel sorry for Macron and the mayor of Paris, as this#JeChieDansLaSeineLe23JuinAKA, "I shit in the Seine on June 23 is indeed a bowel movementhttps://t.co/WcB7K2AelVpic.twitter.com/41TLOpdaMY

President Emmanuel Macron has also promised to swim in the river before the Games but has not specified a date.

On Friday, Paris region prefect Marc Guillaume said at a press conference the Seine was still too dirty to swim and there was “no doubt” it was not “up to par” with standards.

It is not clear whether the Seine will be fit for use in the Olympics triathlon competition.

However, multiple swimming spots along the Seine are expected to be opened in the summer of 2025 in Paris.

title: Angry Parisians threaten to poo in the Seine to protest cost of cleaning it before the Olympics, forcing mayor Anne Hidalgo to delay her plunge into the river | Sky News Australia
description: The mayor of the French capital has postponed her plunge into the Seine after furious Parisians threatened to defecate in it to protest against the billions spent cleaning the iconic river ahead of the Olympics next month.
